reference.response:
## Header
- **Program name:** Home Dumbbell Hypertrophy + Body Recomp (4-Day Upper/Lower for Beginners)  
- **Program Owner:** GymText  
- **User:** Rachel Green  
- **Goal:** Build muscle (hypertrophy focus) while improving body composition (fat loss/recomp) and reducing stress  
- **Duration:** Ongoing with phase cycling  

---

## Program Philosophy
Rachel is a beginner training at home with dumbbells, bands, and a pull-up bar, so this program uses a simple **4-day upper/lower split** to build muscle efficiently without needing barbells or machines. The plan emphasizes **repeatable movement patterns** (squat/lunge, hinge, push, pull, core/carry) with enough weekly volume to drive hypertrophy while keeping sessions predictable and stress-friendly. Because fat loss is partly nutrition-driven and training recovery matters, we’ll use **moderate effort (RPE-based)**, a small amount of optional low-impact conditioning, and built-in deloads to prevent burnout. Progression is primarily **double progression** (add reps first, then load) with clear RPE targets so you can adjust to whatever dumbbells you have available.

---

# Phase 1: Foundation & Technique (Weeks 1–4)

### Weekly Pattern
**Mon — Upper A (Push/Pull Basics)**
- **Focus:** Pressing and rowing mechanics + upper-back volume
- **Volume:** ~14–18 working sets

1) Dumbbell Floor Press: 3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 7**  
- *floor press limits shoulder range, beginner-friendly pressing stability*  
2) One-Arm Dumbbell Row (bench/couch supported): 3 × 8–12/side @ **RPE 7**  
3) Band Lat Pulldown (anchor high) **or** Assisted Pull-Up (band): 3 × 6–10 @ **RPE 7**  
- *vertical pulling to build lats and support posture/shoulders*  
4) Dumbbell Shoulder Press (seated or standing): 2 × 8–12 @ **RPE 7**  
5) Band Face Pull: 2 × 12–20 @ **RPE 7**  
- *rear delts/external rotation for shoulder balance*  
6) Dumbbell Curl: 2 × 10–15 @ **RPE 7**  
7) Band Triceps Pressdown **or** Overhead Band Extension: 2 × 10–15 @ **RPE 7**

---

**Tue — Lower A (Squat + Glute Emphasis)**
- **Focus:** Knee-dominant strength + glutes + trunk stability
- **Volume:** ~14–18 working sets

1) Goblet Squat: 3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 7**  
- *teaches squat pattern with an upright torso; easy to load safely at home*  
2) Romanian Deadlift (DB RDL): 3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 7**  
3) Bulgarian Split Squat (rear foot elevated optional): 2–3 × 8–12/side @ **RPE 7**  
- *single-leg work builds legs with lighter dumbbells—ideal for home loading limits*  
4) Glute Bridge / Hip Thrust (DB on hips): 2–3 × 10–15 @ **RPE 7**  
5) Standing Calf Raise (DBs): 2 × 10–20 @ **RPE 7**  
6) Dead Bug **or** Front Plank: 2 × 20–40 sec @ controlled effort  
- *core stiffness for better squats/hinges and lower stress/low fatigue*

---

**Thu — Upper B (Back Priority + Arms/Delts)**
- **Focus:** More pulling volume, shoulder/arm hypertrophy
- **Volume:** ~14–18 working sets

1) Incline Dumbbell Press (or feet-elevated push-up if no incline): 3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 7**  
2) Pull-Up Progression (choose one):  
   - Assisted Pull-Up (band): 3 × 4–8 @ **RPE 7–8**  
   - **or** Negative Pull-Ups: 3 × 3–6 slow lowers (3–5 sec)  
- *direct skill practice for the pull-up bar while building lats/upper back*  
3) Two-DB Row (hinged) **or** Band Seated Row: 3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 7**  
4) Dumbbell Lateral Raise: 2–3 × 12–20 @ **RPE 7**  
5) Band Pull-Apart: 2 × 12–25 @ **RPE 7**  
6) Hammer Curl: 2 × 10–15 @ **RPE 7**  
7) Overhead Triceps Extension (DB or band): 2 × 10–15 @ **RPE 7**

---

**Sat — Lower B (Hinge + Hamstrings + Conditioning Finish)**
- **Focus:** Posterior chain hypertrophy + optional sweat/stress relief
- **Volume:** ~14–18 working sets (+ optional finisher)

1) Dumbbell Deadlift (suitcase or two-DB): 3 × 6–10 @ **RPE 7**  
- *hinge strength with a slightly heavier feel than RDL*  
2) Step-Up (knee height you can control): 2–3 × 8–12/side @ **RPE 7**  
3) Banded Leg Curl (anchor low) **or** Sliding Hamstring Curl (towels on floor): 3 × 10–15 @ **RPE 7**  
- *adds hamstring knee-flexion work that DB-only setups often miss*  
4) Goblet Squat (lighter) **or** Wall Sit: 2 × 12–20 @ **RPE 7**  
5) Side Plank: 2 × 20–40 sec/side  

**Optional 6–10 min finisher (choose 1, easy-to-moderate):**  
- EMOM (every minute): 8 minutes = 10 bodyweight squats + 8 incline push-ups  
- Or brisk stair walks / fast walk outside if available  
- *kept short to support body comp without spiking fatigue/stress*

---

## Progression Strategy (Phase 1)
**Main method: Double progression + RPE cap**
- For most lifts with a rep range (e.g., 8–12):  
  1) Start with a weight you can do near the **low end** at **RPE 6–7**.  
  2) Each week, add **1–2 reps per set** until you hit the **top of the range** on all sets at **RPE ≤8**.  
  3) Then increase load (smallest jump available) and return to the low end of the range.

**Pull-up progression**
- Keep 3 sets. When you can hit the top of the rep target with a given band, move to a lighter band (or add a 4th set).

**Deload (Week 4)**
- Keep the same exercises, reduce total sets by ~40–50% (e.g., 3 sets → 2; 2 sets → 1) and keep effort around **RPE 6–7**.  
- Transition to Phase 2 after Week 4 (or repeat Phase 1 if form still feels inconsistent).

---

# Phase 2: Hypertrophy Build (Weeks 5–8)

### Weekly Pattern
Same days to reduce decision fatigue and support consistency.

**Mon — Upper A (Heavier Push/Pull)**
- **Focus:** Slightly lower reps on main lifts; progressive overload
- **Volume:** ~14–18 sets  
1) DB Floor Press: 4 × 6–10 @ **RPE 7–8**  
2) One-Arm DB Row: 4 × 6–10/side @ **RPE 7–8**  
3) Assisted Pull-Up / Band Pulldown: 3 × 6–10 @ **RPE 7–8**  
4) DB Shoulder Press: 2 × 8–12 @ **RPE 7–8**  
5) Face Pull: 2 × 12–20 @ **RPE 7**  
6) Curl + Triceps (superset): 2 × 10–15 each @ **RPE 7**

**Tue — Lower A (Squat Pattern Priority)**
- **Volume:** ~14–18 sets  
1) Goblet Squat: 4 × 6–10 @ **RPE 7–8**  
2) DB RDL: 3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 7–8**  
3) Bulgarian Split Squat: 3 × 8–12/side @ **RPE 7–8**  
4) Hip Thrust/Bridge: 2 × 10–15 @ **RPE 7–8**  
5) Calf Raise: 2 × 12–20 @ **RPE 7**  
6) Plank variation: 2 sets

**Thu — Upper B (Volume/Delts/Back)**
- **Volume:** ~14–20 sets  
1) Incline DB Press: 3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 7–8**  
2) Pull-Up Progression: 4 × 4–8 @ **RPE 7–8**  
3) Row variation (DB or band): 3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 7–8**  
4) Lateral Raise: 3 × 12–20 @ **RPE 7–8**  
5) Pull-Apart: 2 × 15–25 @ **RPE 7**  
6) Arms (curl + triceps): 2–3 × 10–15 @ **RPE 7–8**

**Sat — Lower B (Hinge + Hamstrings)**
- **Volume:** ~14–18 sets  
1) DB Deadlift: 4 × 6–10 @ **RPE 7–8**  
2) Step-Up: 3 × 8–12/side @ **RPE 7–8**  
3) Hamstring Curl (band/slider): 3 × 10–15 @ **RPE 7–8**  
4) Squat accessory (lighter goblet or wall sit): 2 × 12–20 @ **RPE 7**  
5) Side plank or Pallof press (band): 2 sets  

### Progression Strategy (Phase 2)
- Continue **double progression**, but allow top sets to reach **RPE 8–9** occasionally on the last set of the first two exercises each day.  
- If dumbbells are “too light” to progress load: slow tempo (e.g., **3-sec lowering**), add a 1–2 sec pause, or add 1 set to the main lift (cap at +1 set per day).
- **Deload (Week 8):** same as Phase 1 deload.

---

# Phase 3: Recomp Sustain + Variety (Weeks 9–12)

### Weekly Pattern
Keep the split but rotate a few movements to reduce overuse and keep motivation high (while still training the same patterns).

**Mon — Upper A (Press/Row Variation)**
- DB Bench (if you have a bench) **or** Push-Up (banded if strong): 3–4 × 6–12 @ **RPE 7–8**  
- Chest-supported DB Row (prone on pillows/bench) **or** 1-arm row: 3–4 × 8–12 @ **RPE 7–8**  
- Band Pulldown / Assisted Pull-Up: 3 × 6–10 @ **RPE 7–8**  
- Lateral raise + face pull: 2–3 sets each  
- Arms: 2 sets each

**Tue — Lower A (Quad/Glute)**
- Heels-elevated Goblet Squat: 3–4 × 8–12 @ **RPE 7–8**  
- DB RDL: 3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 7–8**  
- Reverse Lunge: 3 × 8–12/side @ **RPE 7–8**  
- Hip thrust: 2–3 × 10–15 @ **RPE 7–8**  
- Core: 2 sets

**Thu — Upper B (Pull-Up Skill + Upper Back)**
- Pull-Up progression: 4 × 3–8 @ **RPE 7–9**  
- Band row or DB row: 3–4 × 8–12 @ **RPE 7–8**  
- DB Shoulder Press: 3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 7–8**  
- Rear delt fly (DB/band): 2–3 × 12–20 @ **RPE 7**  
- Arms: 2–3 sets each

**Sat — Lower B (Hinge/Hamstrings + Finisher)**
- DB Deadlift or Suitcase Deadlift: 3–4 × 6–10 @ **RPE 7–8**  
- Step-up: 3 × 8–12/side @ **RPE 7–8**  
- Slider/band ham curl: 3 × 10–15 @ **RPE 7–8**  
- Calves + core: 2 sets each  
- Optional finisher 6–12 minutes (same concept as Phase 1)

### Progression Strategy (Phase 3)
- Use **performance targets**: aim to beat Week 9 by Week 11 in at least one way each session (more reps, better control/tempo, slightly heavier DB, or an extra set on one accessory).  
- **Deload (Week 12):** reduce sets 40–50%, keep RPE 6–7.

---

## Phase Cycling
- Cycle in this order: **Foundation (4 wks) → Build (4 wks) → Sustain/Variety (4 wks) → repeat**.  
- Repeat the cycle when:  
  - You complete the deload week feeling recovered, and  
  - Key lifts have stalled for ~2–3 weeks (a fresh phase helps), or  
  - Stress/sleep is worse than usual (stay in Foundation/Sustain an extra 4 weeks).  
- Over time, your “Build” phase becomes the main driver; “Sustain” keeps progress moving while supporting adherence and stress reduction.

---

## Modification History
- **2026-02-27:** Program created for home training (DBs/bands/pull-up bar), 4-day schedule, hypertrophy + body recomposition focus, beginner-friendly RPE and deload structure.

--- 

If you tell me (1) your dumbbell max weights and whether you have an adjustable bench, and (2) how many assisted pull-ups or dead-hang seconds you can do, I can tighten the rep ranges and progressions to match your exact loading options.
